affected_files.reason: TestSplitAtomicCommit's 4 crash-point subtests (crashPointTest) now reopen brand-new *catalog.Catalog/*btree.Tree/*graph.EdgeAppender objects rooted at the same on-disk locations before calling RecoverSplitCommits, instead of reusing deps.cat/deps.tree/deps.appender in place. Added atomicCommitTestDeps.catalogPath/treeIndexPath/edgesDir fields, reopenFreshSplitDeps helper, newTestBtreeWithPath/openTestBtreeAt helpers (refactored out of newTestBtree), and fixed putSplittingRecord to seed its StatusSplitting record through a WAL-covered cat.Put (required precondition for fresh-catalog reconstruction to find it via catalog.RecoverFromWAL).

not_touched: engine/split/execute.go (no production code change needed; RecoverSplitCommits' contract and doc comment already correctly describe what it does/does not reconstruct)
risk_notes: Test-only change; no production code path affected.
putSplittingRecord signature changed (added *wal.Writer param) but has exactly one call site (TestSplitAtomicCommit's newDeps), updated in the same commit.

reopenFreshSplitDeps opens a second *catalog.FileManager and a second btree NodeStore/NodeAllocator against the same on-disk files deps already has open; verified no O_EXCL/exclusive-lock semantics in catalog.Open/btree.OpenIndexFile that would conflict with the original still-open handles (both use os.OpenFile with O_RDWR|O_CREATE, no locking).

Concurrent-agent collision risk: other subtasks under issue #40 (4.5.3.2 guard.go eviction, 4.5.3.3 orchestrate.go abandoned-SPLITTING recovery) are being worked on concurrently in the same shared git index; per protocol, if the eventual commit sweeps in unrelated staged changes from those, this will be disclosed in the final report/handoff rather than treated as an error or fixed via git-history rewriting.
